frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Arre bitiya, ladki ko samjha do, aajkal zamaana bahut kharab hai. Itni raat tak bahar rehna, akeli aana-jana theek nahi hota.
B: Par galti uski thodi na hai.
A: Galti ki baat nahi kar rahi, par apna bachav bhi to aurat ko hi dekhna padta hai. Mard ki fitrat badalne mein time lagega, isliye sambhal ke rehna hi samajhdari hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames male violence as inevitable and places the burden of safety on women to restrict their own movement. It uses protective, practical advice language ('zamaana kharab hai', 'sambhal ke rehna') rather than directly blaming men.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit and his wife Priya have been married four years. Priya works at a private company and comes home tired. She orders food from Zomato two or three times a week. You never say anything directly to Priya about it. But today your daughter called from Delhi and asked how things are going and you are telling her. You are comparing Priya's cooking habits to how you cooked for this family for 35 years and what you sacrificed and what it means when a daughter in law does not cook and what kind of home that makes.
